channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How execs can bridge the AI knowledge gap
item.description: From streamlining administrative tasks to enhancing brainstorming sessions, AI is becoming an essential workplace companion. Yet, despite its transformative promise, its integration isn’t as simple as flipping a switch.
item.dc:creator: Dave Grow, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Artists can copyright works made with AI assistance: Agency
item.description: Artists can copyright work they create using tools powered by artificial intelligence, as long as sufficient human creativity is involved, according to a new report from the U.S. Copyright Office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The cheapest 2TB external SSD ever costs less an equivalent internal SSD, reaches speeds of 1GB/s and I can't see a catch
item.description: This 2TB Patriot Transporter SSD stands out for its affordability and impressive read/write speeds of up to 1GB/s.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: After DeepSeek, the AI giants still have plenty of work left to do
item.description: The tech industry has long been infatuated with metaphors relating to the original space race. Its favorite one is moon shot, a term it applies to any undertaking of atypical ambition.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: AI-assisted works can get copyright with enough human creativity, says US copyright office
item.description: An AI-assisted work could be copyrightable if an artist’s handiwork is perceptible. A human adapting an AI-generated output with “creative arrangements or modifications” could also make it fall under copyright protections.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025
item.description: Last year was an eventful one on the cybersecurity front. But big cyber developments in the federal scape are expected in 2025. The post Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI investigating whether DeepSeek improperly obtained data
item.description: OpenAI is examining whether Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup DeepSeek improperly obtained data from its models to build a popular new AI assistant, a spokesperson confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Assessing the Future for AI PCs – What IT Execs are Saying
item.description: Over the past couple of years, AI has infiltrated virtually every aspect of digital infrastructure, with an initial focus on storage and compute servers. Now, as organizations move from proofs of concept to deployment of AI projects, interest is growing in the potential that the AI PC holds.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Every Marvel Easter egg and reference in Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man episodes 1 and 2: Nico Minoru, Lonnie Lincoln, and more
item.description: Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man's first two episodes are out now – here are all of the Marvel references I found.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: iPhones on T-Mobile can access Starlink satellite communications
item.description: Starlink support appears to be a secret iOS 18.3 feature for iPhones, some of which now support satellite communication services from SpaceX — but only for users invited to join public beta testing of the service on US carrier T-Mobile.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Confirmed: the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra won't get a Bluetooth-enabled S Pen after all
item.description: Despite conflicting reports, Samsung has confirmed that the Galaxy S25 Ultra will not support Bluetooth S Pen functionality.
item.dc:creator: axel.metz@futurenet.com (Axel Metz), TechRadar

item.title: Tecnología y negocio en el ámbito industrial, la convergencia de dos imprescindibles para Cerealto
item.description: Sobre los hombros de Juan Manuel García Dujo, actual Chief Information Officer (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) de Cerealto, recae la misión de alinear las TI con los objetivos de negocio.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UK regulator may investigate AWS and Microsoft cloud businesses
item.description: AWS and Microsoft could potentially face an investigation of their cloud businesses in Britain should the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) follow the recommendation of an independent inquiry group that reports into the UK’s antitrust regulator.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stable values API would speed Java startups
item.description: Startup times for Java applications would be improved through a proposed API for stable values, which are objects that hold immutable data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Linux gets support for the Copilot key
item.description: There is a special Copilot key on some Windows laptops that can be used to launch Microsoft’s AI assistant — and now support for the key is coming to Linux.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple and the art of IT management
item.description: They may be stressed about shadow IT, security, endpoint management, and the recent proliferation of artificial intelligence (AI) tools. They’re challenged not just by static budgets and rising costs, but by the proliferation of tools they now must use to do their job.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fake toll road texts sweep America as Chinese scammers target US drivers
item.description: Toll road text scam: Fake messages claim unpaid fees, seek payment via fraudulent links. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this scam is becoming increasingly sophisticated and widespread.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: DeepSeek 'wake-up call' for US tech giants
item.description: President Trump said Monday that DeepSeek, a Chinese startup behind a popular new artificial intelligence (AI) model, represented a “wake-up call” for American tech firms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: DeepSeek triggers shock waves for AI giants, but the disruption won’t last
item.description: Chinese start-up DeepSeek rocked the tech industry Monday after the company’s new generative AI (genAI) bot hit Apple’s App Store and Google Play Store and downloads almost immediately exceeded those of OpenAI’s ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Prompt Security adds code sanitization, data leak prevention for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Prompt Security has announced an upgrade to its security and governance platform for GitHub Copilot and other AI coding assistants.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: iPhone users turn on to DeepSeek AI
item.description: As if from nowhere, OpenAI competitor DeepSeek has somersaulted to the top of the iPhone App Store chart, overtaking ChatGPT’s OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ready or not, here it comes: GenAI in 2025
item.description: If you’re a software developer right now, it is nearly impossible to avoid chatter about generative AI. You’re probably already using AI tools in your work, whether you like it or not. Some of your normie friends are using ChatGPT as a search engine, with hilarious and alarming results.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? This is probably why
item.description: The official POTUS and White House accounts are managed by the White House and “change when the occupant of the White House changes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5
